senses.definition: a type of golf club designed specifically for use on the putting green to make short and precise strokes aimed at getting the ball into the hole

examples.x: The golfer used a putter to sink the ball into the hole from the edge of the green.
During the tournament, he relied on his trusty putter to navigate the tricky greens.
The putter's flat face is designed to provide a smooth and accurate roll on the green.
The coach gave tips on how to grip the putter for better control and precision.
Every golfer carries a putter in their bag for those crucial short-distance shots.

senses.definition: a sports equipment made of fiberglass, used for vaulting over a high bar

examples.x: The pole snapped during the competition, but the athlete was unharmed.
He soared over the bar with the help of his fiberglass pole.
The athlete gripped the pole tightly before launching into the air.
After practicing with different poles, he finally found the right one for his height.
The coach emphasized the importance of the pole’s flexibility in pole vaulting.

senses.definition: an object that allows a person or thing to slowly come to the ground after dropping from a flying aircraft
the main fabric portion of the parachute that inflates and slows the descent of the skydiver

examples.x: His canopy had colorful panels that fluttered in the wind.
The canopy filled with air quickly, slowing their descent.
The canopy deployed smoothly after he pulled the ripcord.
They practiced emergency procedures for canopy failures during training.
She landed softly on the ground under the canopy after a successful jump.

senses.definition: a big, colorful, and light kite that people use to fly in the sky

examples.x: The paraglider was packed carefully into the van after the flight.
They stored the paraglider in the garage when it wasn't in use.
The paraglider's wing inflated smoothly as the wind picked up.
After landing, she folded the paraglider and stored it in its bag.
The paraglider's design made it easy to control and steer during the flight.

senses.definition: a light, non-motorized glider aircraft designed for foot-launched flight

examples.x: The adventurer launched his hang glider from the cliff, soaring high above the valley.
During the summer, she took lessons to learn how to fly a hang glider safely.
The hang glider's fabric wings caught the air, allowing the pilot to glide gracefully.
The hang glider's lightweight frame made it easy to carry to the top of the launch site.
The instructor emphasized safety procedures before the group took off with their hang gliders.

senses.definition: a type of sports equipment used in kiteboarding and kite landboarding, designed to generate significant pull from the wind

examples.x: The power kite soared gracefully in the sky.
He flew his power kite high above the beach.
She controlled the power kite with skill and precision.
We used a power kite to practice kite surfing.
Learning to launch a power kite can be challenging for beginners.

senses.definition: a type of kite designed for precision flying and performing tricks, often controlled by multiple lines for enhanced control

examples.x: The enthusiast flew his sport kite on the beach, executing intricate tricks and loops.
During the festival, spectators marveled at the synchronized performance of multiple sport kites.
The sport kite's lightweight frame and durable fabric allowed for high-speed stunts.
Every flyer needs to master basic skills before attempting advanced maneuvers with a sport kite.
At the kite shop, customers can find a variety of sport kites suited for different skill levels.

senses.definition: a type of powered paraglider used in adventure sports like paramotoring and powered paragliding

examples.x: He strapped on his paramotor and soared into the sky with a gentle hum of the engine.
Paramotors allow enthusiasts to take off and land from almost anywhere with ease.
The paramotor's lightweight frame makes it portable and convenient for travel.
He competed in paramotor races, navigating through challenging courses at high speeds.
During the summer, paramotors dot the skies as enthusiasts take advantage of ideal flying conditions.

senses.definition: a strong, flexible cord used in sports like rock climbing and mountaineering to secure climbers

examples.x: The climber tied the climbing rope securely to her harness.
Make sure to check the climbing rope for any signs of wear before each use.
He rappelled down the cliff using a thick climbing rope.
Climbing ropes come in various lengths and thicknesses for different purposes.
Always double-check your knots when using a climbing rope.

senses.definition: a type of sports equipment used in ice climbing to secure climbers to the ice

examples.x: Make sure to place the ice screw securely before continuing the climb.
He carefully twisted the ice screw into the frozen waterfall.
Ice screws come in various lengths to accommodate different ice thicknesses.
She carried a dozen ice screws in her climbing pack.
The climber's ice screw held firm despite the slippery conditions.

senses.definition: a spiked attachment for boots used to prevent slipping on snow and ice during activities like mountaineering and ice climbing

examples.x: The climber strapped crampons onto his boots before tackling the icy slope.
Every serious ice climber invests in quality crampons for safety and stability in alpine conditions.
The crampon's steel spikes provided traction on the steep, frozen terrain.
The experienced hiker demonstrated how to adjust crampons for a snug fit on different boot types.
The guide checked everyone's crampons before starting the ascent to ensure they were securely fastened.

senses.definition: a metal clip used in activities like rock climbing to quickly and securely connect ropes and gear

examples.x: The climber clipped the carabiner to his harness before scaling the cliff.
The carabiner's strong construction ensures reliable support when securing ropes.
She packed a few lightweight carabiners in her backpack for the hiking and climbing trip.
Every rock climber carries several carabiners for safety and ease of attaching equipment.
During the expedition, she used multiple carabiners to connect her gear to the anchor points.

senses.definition: a type of sports equipment used in skeleton racing, designed for an athlete to slide headfirst down an icy track

examples.x: She practiced her starts repeatedly on the skeleton sled.
The skeleton sled is streamlined for maximum speed.
The athlete wore a specialized helmet while using the skeleton sled.
Competitors must maneuver the skeleton sled through challenging turns.
The skeleton sled is made of lightweight materials for agility.

senses.definition: a type of sports equipment used in wakeboarding to secure the rider's feet to the wakeboard

examples.x: He adjusted his wakeboard binding before heading out onto the lake.
The new wakeboard binding provided excellent ankle support.
Her old wakeboard binding was starting to wear out.
The wakeboard binding came loose during his jump.
These wakeboard bindings are designed for maximum comfort and control.

senses.definition: a piece of exercise equipment that consists of a stretchy cloth attached to a frame on which people can jump for fun or exercise

examples.x: The children spent the afternoon bouncing on the trampoline in the backyard.
Gymnasts use the trampoline to practice their aerial maneuvers and flips.
Safety nets around the trampoline help prevent accidents while jumping.
The trampoline park was filled with people of all ages enjoying the various bouncing activities.
After installing a trampoline in their garden, the family noticed an increase in their outdoor playtime.

senses.definition: a sports equipment used in archery to assist in releasing the bowstring accurately and smoothly

examples.x: Make sure your release aid is properly calibrated before the competition.
She prefers using a wrist strap release aid for better control.
The archer adjusted the tension on his release aid for a more consistent shot.
His accuracy improved significantly after switching to a thumb trigger release aid.
The release aid slipped from his fingers, causing him to miss the target.

senses.definition: a container used by climbers to hold powdered chalk for drying and improving grip on climbing holds

examples.x: His chalk bag was filled with fresh chalk for the bouldering competition.
She clipped her chalk bag to her harness before starting the climb.
The chalk bag hung from a hook on the wall of the indoor climbing gym.
The chalk bag slipped off the cliff edge during her climb.
The chalk bag was embroidered with her initials as a gift from her climbing partner.

senses.definition: a wire used in fencing to connect the foil, epee, or saber to the scoring apparatus

examples.x: Make sure your body cord is securely fastened before starting the match.
The fencer's body cord malfunctioned during the bout, causing a delay.
She replaced her old body cord with a new, more durable one.
His body cord tangled during the intense match, requiring him to pause.
The referee inspected each fencer's body cord before the competition began.

senses.definition: a heavy metal ball attached to a grip by a steel wire, used by athletes to throw for distance in track and field competitions

examples.x: The athlete swung the hammer overhead before releasing it in the hammer throw event.
The hammer's solid construction and weight distribution help generate momentum during the spin.
He celebrated a new personal best after launching the hammer beyond the previous record distance.
The hammer's handle is specifically designed for a secure grip during the rotational technique.
The young athlete trained tirelessly to perfect the release timing of the hammer.

senses.definition: a seat made for riding on the back of a horse or other animal

examples.x: She adjusted the saddle before mounting her horse.
The old cowboy cleaned his worn leather saddle after a long ride.
The saddle was uncomfortable at first, but she got used to it.
He placed the saddle on the horse and tightened the girth strap.
A good saddle is essential for long-distance horseback riding.

senses.definition: a protective covering used in sports such as kayaking or canoeing to shield the cockpit area from water

examples.x: The kayak's cockpit cover kept the interior dry during transport.
After paddling, he removed the cockpit cover to drain any collected water.
The cockpit cover prevented leaves and insects from getting inside the kayak.
She secured the cockpit cover tightly before launching her canoe.
Before the race, the athletes checked their cockpit covers for any tears or damage.

senses.definition: the barrier in the middle of a court over which players hit the ball, used in sports such as tennis

examples.x: The tennis player's powerful serve sent the ball over the net and into the opponent's court.
She reached the net quickly to volley the ball back to her opponent's side.
The net divides the tennis court into two halves and determines where the ball can land during a rally.
The ball hit the top of the net and bounced over, earning the player a point.
They adjusted the tension of the net to ensure it was set at the proper height for the match.

senses.definition: the vertical bar or beam that serves as one of the two upright supports for the crossbar in sports such as football, soccer, and field hockey
a pair of upright posts typically used in sports such as football or soccer to define the area where goals are scored

examples.x: The goalkeeper made an incredible save, preventing the ball from hitting the goalpost.
The ball bounced off the goalpost and back into play during the soccer match.
The rugby player kicked the ball between the goalposts to score three points.
He aimed for the center of the goalpost, but his shot went wide.
After the renovation, the new goalposts were much sturdier.

senses.definition: the seat where the rider sits, designed to provide comfort and support during cycling

examples.x: The cyclist adjusted the saddle height for a comfortable riding position.
Every cyclist chooses a saddle that suits their riding style and body shape.
During the bike tour, she appreciated the cushioned saddle for long-distance comfort.
The coach recommended a saddle tilt adjustment to improve the rider's pedaling efficiency.
He upgraded his bike with a lightweight saddle to enhance performance during races.

senses.definition: a metal mouthpiece and its attached components used in a bridle to control a horse

examples.x: During the competition, she used gentle cues with the bit to communicate with her horse.
The bit's design and placement affect how the horse responds to the rider's commands.
The rider adjusted the bit in the horse's mouth for better control during the training session.

senses.definition: a device placed on a horse's head, used by a rider to guide and control the horse's movements

examples.x: The rider adjusted the bridle carefully before mounting the horse for their ride.
The leather of the bridle creaked softly as the horse turned its head.
The horse tossed its head, resisting the pressure of the bridle as the rider attempted to guide it.
The bridle was adorned with silver buckles and intricate designs, reflecting the owner's wealth and status.
Before the race, the jockey checked that the bridle was securely fastened to ensure maximum control over the horse.

senses.definition: a strap or band that goes around the horse's belly to secure the saddle in place

examples.x: The rider tightened the girth to ensure the saddle was securely fastened before mounting.
The girth's material and design are chosen for durability and comfort for the horse.
Every equestrian checks the girth for proper fit and tightness before riding.
During the trail ride, she adjusted the girth to keep the saddle comfortable for her horse.
She cleaned and maintained the girth regularly to ensure it remained in good condition.

senses.definition: a piece of equipment for horses designed to control the horse's head position and prevent it from raising its head too high

examples.x: The rider adjusted the martingale to help maintain the horse's head in a steady position during the ride.
The martingale's design ensures the horse's head remains within a desired range of motion.
Every jumper or eventing rider may use a martingale to help with control and stability over jumps.
She checked the martingale's fit and condition before every ride to ensure proper function.
During training, she used the martingale to improve the horse's responsiveness to rein cues.

senses.definition: a structural piece of rigging on a sailboat that supports the bowsprit

examples.x: The sailor adjusted the martingale to ensure the bowsprit remained secure during the rough seas.
Every sailor understands the importance of inspecting and maintaining the martingale for safe navigation.
The martingale's sturdy design provided extra support to the bow area of the sailboat.
The martingale's tension on the rigging helped to optimize the sailboat's performance in varying wind conditions.
He appreciated the martingale's contribution to the sailboat's stability and efficiency while cruising offshore.

senses.definition: a strap or rope attached to a bridle, used by a rider to control a horse

examples.x: She gently pulled on the rein to guide the horse to the left.
His hands held the reins lightly as they trotted along the trail.
The reins were held firmly during the jump to maintain control.
He gripped the reins tightly as the horse galloped across the field.
The rider adjusted the rein length for better communication with the horse.

senses.definition: a piece of equipment worn around a horse's chest and shoulders

examples.x: The breastplate's design and fit are crucial for comfort and freedom of movement for the horse.
She cleaned and conditioned the breastplate regularly to maintain its durability and appearance.
The young rider's horse wore a padded leather breastplate for long-distance trail rides.
The breastplate's adjustable straps allowed for a customized fit according to the horse's build.
The instructor recommended using a breastplate to enhance the horse's comfort and performance during lessons.

senses.definition: a short whip-like tool used by riders to cue and communicate with horses during riding

examples.x: The jockey used a riding crop to encourage the horse to accelerate during the race.
During training, she used the riding crop to give precise cues to her horse.
The young rider's riding crop had a padded end for softer cues on sensitive horses.
The riding crop's gentle tap reminded the horse to maintain stride and focus.
Every rider should use a riding crop responsibly, understanding its purpose as a training aid.

senses.definition: a small metal tool worn on the rider's heel that is used to give subtle commands to a horse

examples.x: The spur's gentle pressure reminded the horse to maintain a steady pace.
Every equestrian must use spurs with care and skill to avoid discomfort for the horse.
The young rider's spurs had rounded ends to provide gentle encouragement to her pony.
She adjusted the spur straps to ensure a snug fit on her riding boots.
He recognized the importance of proper spur use in refining the horse's responses during competitions.

senses.definition: a solid wooden or metal rod, used on sailing ships to secure ropes or lines

examples.x: The sailor secured the main sail using a belaying pin on the ship's deck.
The belaying pin's sturdy construction ensured the ropes stayed in place during maneuvers.
On the sailing vessel, sailors are trained to quickly belay lines using various sizes of belaying pins.
The captain inspected the belaying pins regularly to ensure they were in good condition.
During the storm, she relied on the belaying pins to manage the rigging in the turbulent seas.

senses.definition: a piece of sports equipment used in equestrian sports to provide cushioning and protect the horse's back

examples.x: The saddle pad was embroidered with intricate designs, adding flair to the riding gear.
He adjusted the saddle pad to ensure it sat comfortably beneath the saddle.
The saddle pad was made of breathable material to keep the horse cool during the ride.
Before the competition, riders inspected their horses' saddle pads for any signs of wear.
The saddle pad absorbed sweat, preventing it from irritating the horse's skin.

senses.definition: the devices used to see distant objects more clearly by looking through two small telescopes mounted together

examples.x: The birdwatcher used binoculars to observe rare species in the treetops.
During the hike, she used binoculars to get a closer look at wildlife in the distance.
At the nature reserve, visitors can rent binoculars for guided birdwatching tours.

senses.definition: a versatile tool used in mountaineering and ice climbing, featuring a pointed pick for anchoring in ice and a spike for stability on snow

examples.x: The climber used his ice ax to secure a hold on the steep, icy slope.
During the ascent, the mountaineer relied on the ice ax to navigate through the glacier.
Every mountaineer carries an ice ax for safety and support in icy and snowy conditions.
The ice ax's pick helped the climber anchor himself while ascending the frozen waterfall.

senses.definition: a pole that sticks out from the front of a sailboat, used to support the front sails

examples.x: During the race, the crew used the bowsprit to hold larger sails.
At the dock, the captain checked the bowsprit for any damage.
The sailor adjusted the sail attached to the bowsprit.
The bowsprit helped balance and steer the sailboat better.
The crew tightened the ropes connected to the bowsprit.
